channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Could Agentic AI be the killer app for the 40-year old PC? AMD thinks so — and wants you to jump on the Agent Computer bandwagon before it is too late
item.description: AMD introduces Agentic AI on PCs, enabling autonomous task execution, persistent local models, and enhanced productivity for professionals and organizations.

item.title: The White House proposes new AI policy framework that supersedes state laws
item.description: The White House has announced a new AI policy framework that calls for Congress to craft federal regulation that overrules state AI laws. The Trump administration has made multiple attempts to overrule more restrictive state-level AI regulation, but has failed so far, most notably in the passing of the “One Big Beautiful Bill.”The framework focuses on a variety of topics, covering everything from child privacy to the use of AI in the workforce. “Importantly, this framework can succeed only if it is applied uniformly across the United States,” The White House writes. “A patchwork of conflicting...

item.title: Everything We Know About the Future of ‘Star Trek’
item.description: As the franchise prepares to celebrate 60 years of boldly going, its future in TV and film remains more in flux than it's been in a decade.

item.title: ‘We are only able to supply, for our key customers in the midterm, about 50% to two-thirds of their requirements’: Micron CEO forecasts production spend increase to meet the insane demand for memory – but the RAM crisis will only get worse
item.description: The RAM crisis is having a knock-on effect on the memory industry in general - but there could be an unlikely hero.

item.title: Amidst legal turmoil, Kalshi is temporarily banned in Nevada
item.description: It's the latest escalation in a regulatory battle over the future of prediction markets.

item.title: Gamers Hate Nvidia's DLSS 5. Developers Aren’t Crazy About It, Either
item.description: Nvidia’s new AI upscaling gaming technology struck gamers as uncanny and off-putting. Developers don't seem to like it, either, but it could be “the default” in a few years.

item.title: Pinterest CEO calls on governments to ban social media for users under 16
item.description: Pinterest’s chief compares social media to tobacco and alcohol, saying children need similar protections.

item.title: Trump Proposes a ‘Light Touch’ National Framework for AI Policy
item.description: The next closest thing to no rules at all.

item.title: Windows 11 is finally getting a movable taskbar
item.description: It's taken almost five years, but Microsoft is finally bringing back the ability to move the Windows taskbar to the top or sides of your screen. The movable taskbar is part of some much bigger changes coming to Windows 11 this year in response to growing criticism of the operating system's performance, reliability, and user […]

item.title: Microsoft is ending the Windows Update nightmare — and letting you pause them indefinitely
item.description: In 2015, Microsoft decided that you shouldn't be in control of updating your PC anymore. At first, it seemed like a good idea to keep malware at bay - but soon, users discovered their computers were automatically shutting down and erasing work in the middle of the day. Then, Microsoft abused its power to install […]

item.title: Businesses are being 'locked in' to all-in-one platforms – and its costing them growth and adaptability
item.description: New research shows that companies are being held back by being locked into single ecosystems – but switching isn't easy.

item.title: This is Microsoft’s plan to fix Windows 11
item.description: Microsoft has faced a breakdown of trust in Windows 11 and a backlash over AI additions to its operating system in recent months. After promising to rebuild trust in Windows earlier this year, Microsoft's Windows chief, Pavan Davuluri, is now revealing the company's plan to fix Windows 11 - and there are a lot of […]

item.title: Three people have been charged with illegally exporting NVIDIA GPUs to China
item.description: The US Attorney's Office for the Southern District of New York has charged three people with illegally exporting NVIDIA GPUs to China in violation of the Export Control Reform Act. NVIDIA's chips have become a critical component in the rush to train and run increasingly complex artificial intelligence models, one the US has sought to manipulate with export controls and profit-sharing schemes with NVIDIA.The three people, Yih-Shyan "Wally" Liaw, Ruei-Tsang "Steven" Chang and Ting-Wei "Willy" Sun, two employees and one contractor working for US IT company Super Micro Computer, allegedly circumve...

item.title: Cet écran PC Acer (QD-OLED et 240 Hz) de 27″ va plaire aux gamers avec 150 € de moins
item.description: Les joueurs aguerris visent toujours des écrans PC bien réactifs et fluides. Ceux-là devraient donc être satisfaits de l'Acer Predator X27UW3bmiiprx, qui coche bien des cases, et qu’on trouve à 449 euros au lieu de 599 euros de base.

item.title: Here's how not to leak military information with your Strava run
item.description: A French officer recently leaked the location of an aircraft carrier because of a run on the sports app Strava. This is not the first time this has happened, as the app tracks location data. It was used to access the location of US military bases back in 2018 and members of the Secret Service accidentally shared their whereabouts while protecting then-US President Joe Biden. The same has happened to President Trump and other world leaders. 🚨🇫🇷 NEW: The location of the French aircraft carrier, FS Charles de Gaulle, has been given away by a sailor using Strava whilst jogging on the ship deck[@le...

item.title: Grid Down? Scientists Say Your EV Could Provide the Needed Backup Power
item.description: If EVs survive the ongoing political fiasco, here’s how we could get the most bang for our buck.

item.title: Marvel’s Upcoming Movies Now Almost Extend Until the Next Decade
item.description: The studio must feel good about its post-'Doomsday'' and 'Secret Wars' world as films now go into 2029.

item.title: Getting to the Airport 3 Hours Early Is No Guarantee You’ll Make Your Flight, as TSA Crisis Worsens
item.description: "I've NEVER seen Hartsfield Jackson this bad," one local reporter in Atlanta wrote.

item.title: Google shares 5 tools to help avoid tax scams
item.description: Google shares its tools to help users avoid scams this tax season.

item.title: Trump takes another shot at dismantling state AI regulation
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday unveiled its new legislative blueprint for AI regulation, and the seven-point plan includes a clear message: The federal government should avoid many AI regulations beyond a set of child safety rules, and it should bar states from messing with the "national strategy to achieve global AI dominance." The plan advises […]...

item.title: Pokémon Pokopia event: How to get Hoppip while you can
item.description: If you want Hoppip to show up in your town, all you have to do is be in the right place at the right time.

item.title: These electronic Polaroids are powered by your phone’s NFC chip
item.description: E Ink displays have expanded beyond e-readers. They're now used in digital photo frames, smartphones, and even the price tags found on grocery store shelves. VidaBay's Classic Plus NFC E-Ink Fridge Magnet - which looks like a Polaroid photo - features the same low power color E Ink screens used in those electronic price tags, […]

item.title: Why societal change and technology may be key to Americans regaining trust in the news media
item.description: Speaking with Fast Company at SXSW, two media experts shared their philosophies on the “future of truth.” Is all hope lost for the future of the news media in the U.S.?

item.title: The MacBook Air M4 might be the best laptop buy today for you, despite the Air M5 being so impressive — here’s why
item.description: Just when I'd decided to grab the MacBook Air M5, something came along that made the Air M4 much better value.

item.title: Test du Roborock Qrevo Curv 2 Flow : le roi des aspirateurs frappe-t-il encore un grand coup ?
item.description: En troquant ses traditionnelles serpillières contre un rouleau de lavage ultra-hygiénique, le Roborock Qrevo Curv 2 Flow signe un virage technologique. Est-ce que c'est réussi ? Réponse dans ce test.

item.title: NASA Is Exploring a Reduced Role for SLS as It Gets Cozier With SpaceX
item.description: The Starship rocket could take on a central role in the Artemis missions, propelling astronauts to lunar orbit and landing them on the surface.

item.title: Look ma, no hands! Jony Ive's design firm invents a hands-free, hard-to-read, costly clock — but it plays sounds, too, at least!
item.description: Famous designer Jonathan Ive has created The Clock, a bedside clock that uses light instead of an analog mechanism to tell the time

item.title: I tested the beginner-friendly Anycubic Kobra X - a 4-color 3D printer that's surprisingly good
item.description: Affordable high-speed multi-color printing on a budget.

item.title: Nine months later, the Trump phone still doesn’t exist
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. Once again, we're waiting for a response. It's been more than a month since two Trump Mobile executives showed me what they claimed was the T1 Phone, and in the […]

item.title: This Compact Bose Soundbar Is $80 Off
item.description: Bose might be known for its noise-canceling headphones, but the brand’s soundbars are pretty solid too.

item.title: 'It has to go beyond a display company': I spoke to the CEO of a TV manufacturer about AI, the monetization of TVs software, and how TV needs to evolve
item.description: Just how can TV evolve? I spoke to to Balaji Krishnan, CEO of Displace to get his thoughts on what needs to happen in the TV market.
